授業目的: The purpose of this course is to provide students with basic studying and writing skills to conduct research, give presentations, and prepare academic papers.
到達目標: By the end of this course students will be able to:1. debate various topics at college level;2. search for relevant literature using library databases and academic journals;3. write academic papers at college level;4. give oral presentations based on their opinions.

第14回:
【Flex Hours】 Online class（On-demand format）《90 minutes》
Watch: Amartya Sen, Democracy and Social Decisions. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UF2TUXa9ym0
第15回:
【Flex Hours】Online class（On-demand format）《90 minutes》
Watch: Amartya Sen, Paradigm lost. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OBw5fJkjXiM
トピック.授業計画1: Students will read, discuss, and write short reports on designated readings.
トピック.授業外学習2: Students are to read designated portion of the assigned reading before coming to class.

教科書/Required texts0: Amartya Sen, 1983. Poverty and Famines. (free digital version available for download.)
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school.By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.General Foundation Courses (Practical Orientation):1. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.2. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.3. Ethical Values: Acquiring it though ‘liberal arts education for human growth’ based on Christian principles.
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note Absent from 1/3 or more classes will result in an F. Students will be graded based on 60% of the standard evaluation (presentation and discussion) andthe "academic" report at the end of the semester (approximately 2500 words). The report will be regarded as a"mini-thesis" and should be formatted and written in a strict manner similar to a graduation thesis.
